title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After meeting Mrs. Adela Chester, Mr. Parker Pyne learns of her deep dissatisfaction with local youth and her rigid, conservative views. The following day, Basil Chester tells Pyne that his mother is so traditional she cannot even accept women wearing trousers. Later, a flamboyant woman from the Mariposa Hotel recognizes Pyne and loudly praises his reputation as a problem-solver, an outburst that causes visible tension with Mrs. Chester.

That evening, Mrs. Chester invites Pyne to a salon to seek his assistance. She reveals that Nina Whitchell suggested Pyne could save Basil from a "disastrous marriage" to Betty Gregg. Mrs. Chester expresses disdain for the modern lifestyle of local artists and women, specifically criticizing a girl she observed wearing flannel pants and drinking cocktails. Pyne suggests that Basil's interest in archaeology is not a problem and advises Mrs. Chester that she is overstepping her parental authority. He encourages her to maintain her own identity as an independent person rather than defining herself solely through her son.

Pyne later speaks with Basil, who complains that his mother is narrow-minded and prejudiced against Betty. Basil explains that Betty attempts to appear more modern whenever his mother is present. At Basil's request, Pyne visits Betty and her sister, Stella, at their sparse villa near the beach. There, he meets Stella, who wears no makeup, and Betty, a red-haired woman with a sharp gaze. Betty serves Pyne a cocktail and playfully asks if he has taken a side in the conflict between the young lovers and their elderly opponents.
